title: The Crust & Crumb Bakery

win.title: Bakery Cataloged!
win.subtitle: The Case of the Scattered Pastries
win.nextToast: Proceed to the Police Station Lineup!

completeDialogue: Fantastic work, Rookie! You cataloged the bakery break-in elements using array loop iteration and specialized functional sorting comparators!
completeHint: All pastry evidence parameters are accounted for and organized.

phases.dialogue: When you have a big group of items to look through, our security console stores them in an Array. Think of an array like a numbered list, where every item on the list is called an element! To look at every single item on the list one by one without missing anything, we use a tool called .forEach(). It lets us grab each individual element and check it out in order. Try typing this command to start inspecting every dessert inside the display case array.
phases.output: 🟢 SUCCESS: Ready to inspect elements inside displayCase!
phases.lesson: Arrays hold ordered listings. The array prototype method .forEach executes iterative operations on separate indices sequentially.
notes: Shattered display case holds dynamic items.

active_hotspots.hover_text: An array container containing elements of shattered pastry proof.

phases.dialogue: Great job! When examining an array of objects, we also need to consider what exactly we're checking for. To ensure I don't forget, I list specific actions I want to perform in a { } after the for-each. To prevent confusion, I use a ; to indicate the end of an action! Because the elements of an array are numbered, it's not easy to go and add a new element in between two others. This would require re-numbering every element that comes after! That's why I usually use array.push(newElement). By pushing the new element onto the end, I don't change the order of the elements already on the list, saving me a lot of effort! Let's grab the name field and add it to our list.
phases.output:
Scanning display case elements...
Added to list: "Cinnamon Swirl"
Added to list: "Strawberry Tart"
Added to list: "Croissant"
🟢 SUCCESS: remainingDessertList updated!
phases.lesson: array.push() dynamically appends structural properties onto the terminal index boundary of target sequences.

active_hotspots.hover_text: Each internal item processed exposes an accessible .name property path.
